project_context.description: A mobile-first cafeteria ordering system for Instituto Pío Baroja. Fast-food style (McDonald's inspired) combined with the institute's green identity. Features English/Spanish toggle, tablet/desktop responsiveness, and a QR code pickup system.

rules: Use 'Outfit' for all headings to achieve a modern, friendly fast-food brand voice.
Use 'Manrope' for body text for optimal legibility.
Headlines must be extremely tight in tracking (tracking-tighter) to look like a modern promo.

layout_and_spacing.philosophy: Generous spacing, massive hit targets, and distinct visual boundaries.
patterns: Bottom Navigation Bar on mobile (sticky at bottom) with bold active states (Panera/McDonald's style).
Desktop/Tablet layout utilizes a sidebar for navigation instead of bottom nav.
Pill-shaped, horizontally scrolling category filters for the product catalog.
Product images should visually break out of their cards (negative margins or absolute positioning) to look appetizing.
Bento grid system for dashboard/order history.
layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Use p-4 to p-6 for inner card paddings, and massive gaps (gap-6 to gap-8) between sections.

buttons.style: rounded-full font-bold shadow-md transition-transform hover:scale-105 active:scale-95

cards.style: bg-surface rounded-3xl shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgb(0,0,0,0.04)] border border-border/50 overflow-hidden
cards.product_card: Flex column, large image area, title, price, and a vibrant '+' add-to-cart button.

inputs.style: rounded-2xl border-border bg-background focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary focus:border-primary text-base font-medium p-4

qr_code_summary.style: Massive high-contrast card, pure white background for the QR code, surrounded by brand green. Must be highly readable for cafeteria scanners.

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: All interactive elements must change background color and slightly scale up.
motion_and_interactions.cart_animation: Micro-interaction when item added (e.g., slight bounce on cart icon).
motion_and_interactions.page_transitions: Fade in and slide up slightly on mount.

hero_background.description: Abstract soft green and warm yellow wavy gradient background for promo banners

product_sandwich.description: Healthy fresh sandwich for product catalog

product_salad.description: Fresh salad bowl for product catalog

product_coffee.description: Coffee paper cup for product catalog

instructions_to_main_agent: You have .js files not .tsx files, so strictly write React components using .jsx/.js syntax.
Implement the UI using pure Tailwind CSS and raw HTML tags for layout. Use Shadcn components for complex interactions (Dialogs, Tabs) but strip default Vercel styling.
Add 'data-testid' to all buttons, inputs, links, and cart action elements (e.g., 'add-to-cart-btn', 'checkout-btn', 'login-submit').
Build the application completely in Spanish by default, but implement a toggle context/state that translates key strings to English.
Create a Bottom Navigation component for mobile sizes, and hide it on tablet/desktop in favor of a sidebar or top header.
For the QR code, use a react-qr-code library or a placeholder styled div that looks scannable if library is not available.
Ensure all text has sufficient contrast, specifically avoiding light green text on white or dark green on dark backgrounds.
Do NOT use placeholder image URLs like via.placeholder.com. Use the ones provided in media_urls.
